content:
AI Agent 工程师岗位要求（JD）
一、 岗位名称

AI Agent 工程师 / LLM Agent 开发工程师 / AI 应用架构师

二、 核心职责

Agent 设计与开发：基于大语言模型（LLM）设计、构建和部署自主AI Agent系统，使其具备规划（Planning）、工具使用（Tool Use）、记忆（Memory）和反思（Reflection）能力。

RAG 系统优化：开发和维护检索增强生成（Retrieval-Augmented Generation）管道，确保Agent能够访问最新的外部知识库。

工具集成：将外部API、数据库、SaaS服务（如Gmail, Notion, GitHub）封装成Agent可调用的工具（Function Calling）。

Prompt 与 推理优化：设计和迭代复杂的提示词（Prompt Engineering），优化模型的推理路径，降低幻觉（Hallucination）和延迟。

评估体系（Eval）建设：搭建自动化测试和评估框架，用于衡量Agent在准确性、毒性、效率和成本方面的表现。

多模态探索（加分项）：探索文本、图像、语音结合的多模态Agent应用场景。

三、 硬性技能要求 (Hard Skills)
1. 编程与工程基础 (必备)

Python: 精通Python编程，熟悉异步编程（Asyncio）。

后端开发: 熟悉 FastAPI / Flask / Django 等Web框架，能够构建高并发的API服务。

数据工程: 熟悉 SQL/NoSQL 数据库，了解向量数据库（Milvus, Pinecone, Qdrant, ChromaDB）。

DevOps: 熟悉 Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD 流程，有云原生（AWS/Azure/GCP）部署经验。

2. 大模型生态与框架 (核心)

LLM 框架: 深入理解并熟练使用 LangChain, LlamaIndex, Semantic Kernel, CrewAI​ 或 AutoGen​ 等主流Agent开发框架。

模型 API: 熟悉 OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), Google (Gemini) 以及开源模型（Llama 3, Qwen, DeepSeek）的API调用与微调。

Prompt 工程: 掌握 Few-shot, Chain-of-Thought (CoT), ReAct, Reflection 等提示词技巧。

3. Agent 核心技术栈

RAG (检索增强生成): 熟悉文档切分（Chunking）、Embedding模型选型、混合检索（Hybrid Search）、重排序（Reranking）等技术。

Function/Tool Calling: 能够将复杂业务逻辑封装为Agent可调用的JSON Schema工具。

Memory 机制: 了解短期记忆（Context Window管理）和长期记忆（Vector Store + 摘要）的实现方案。

Agent 架构: 熟悉 ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, 多Agent协作（Multi-Agent Systems）等架构模式。

4. 评估与监控 (Critical)

熟悉 LangSmith, Weights & Biases (W&B), Arize AI​ 等LLMOps工具。

能够定义评估指标（如 Faithfulness, Answer Relevance, Tool Selection Accuracy）。

四、 软技能与综合素质

第一性原理思维：能够从底层逻辑拆解复杂问题，不局限于现有工具的封装。

快速学习能力：LLM领域技术迭代极快，需要每周跟进最新的论文（ArXiv）、开源项目和博客。

产品思维：不仅关注技术实现，更关注用户体验和最终的业务价值（降本增效）。

调试能力：具备极强的耐心和逻辑推理能力，能够调试非确定性（Non-deterministic）系统的错误。

五、 加分项 (Nice to Have)

开源贡献：在 GitHub 上有高质量的 LLM 相关项目，或为知名框架贡献过代码。

学术背景：发表过 NLP/AI 相关顶会论文（ACL, EMNLP, NeurIPS）。

特定领域知识：金融、法律、医疗或代码生成（Code Interpreter）领域的专业知识。

前端能力：熟悉 React/Vue，能够开发 Agent 的交互界面（如 Chatbot UI）。

模型微调：有 Lora, QLora, SFT 等模型微调实战经验，能针对特定任务优化小模型。

processed.job_summary: Design, build, and deploy AI Agent systems based on large language models (LLMs) with planning, tool use, memory, and reflection capabilities. Optimize retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines, integrate external APIs, and improve prompt and reasoning. Build evaluation frameworks and explore multimodal applications.
key_responsibilities: Design and develop AI Agent systems based on LLMs, enabling planning, tool use, memory, and reflection.
Develop and maintain RAG pipelines to access external knowledge bases.
Integrate external APIs, databases, and SaaS services as agent-callable tools (Function Calling).
Design and iterate complex prompts to optimize model reasoning, reduce hallucination and latency.
Build automated testing and evaluation frameworks for agent accuracy, toxicity, efficiency, and cost.
Explore multimodal agent applications (text, image, speech) as a bonus.

required: Proficient in Python, including asynchronous programming (Asyncio).
Familiar with web frameworks such as FastAPI, Flask, or Django; able to build high-concurrency API services.
Proficient in SQL/NoSQL databases; knowledge of vector databases (Milvus, Pinecone, Qdrant, ChromaDB).
Familiar with Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD; experience with cloud-native deployment (AWS/Azure/GCP).
Deep understanding and proficiency in LLM frameworks: LangChain, LlamaIndex, Semantic Kernel, CrewAI, or AutoGen.
Familiar with API calls and fine-tuning of OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), Google (Gemini), and open-source models (Llama 3, Qwen, DeepSeek).
Mastery of prompt engineering techniques: Few-shot, Chain-of-Thought (CoT), ReAct, Reflection.
Knowledge of RAG technologies: chunking, embedding model selection, hybrid search, reranking.
Ability to encapsulate complex business logic as JSON Schema tools for Function Calling.
Understanding of memory mechanisms: short-term (context window management) and long-term (vector store + summarization).
Familiarity with agent architectures: ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, Multi-Agent systems.
Familiar with LLMOps tools: LangSmith, Weights & Biases, Arize AI.
Ability to define evaluation metrics: Faithfulness, Answer Relevance, Tool Selection Accuracy.
First-principles thinking: break down complex problems from fundamental logic.
Rapid learning ability: keep up with latest papers, open-source projects, and blogs weekly.
Product mindset: focus on user experience and business value.
Strong debugging skills: patience and logical reasoning to debug non-deterministic systems.
preferred: Open-source contributions: high-quality LLM projects on GitHub or contributions to well-known frameworks.
Academic background: publications in top NLP/AI conferences (ACL, EMNLP, NeurIPS).
Domain expertise in finance, legal, medical, or code generation.
Frontend skills: familiar with React/Vue; ability to develop agent interaction interfaces (e.g., Chatbot UI).
Model fine-tuning experience: Lora, QLora, SFT; ability to optimize small models for specific tasks.
